methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Standard Subsea, a seed-stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Christopher Amidon, has raised $10M+ to date. The private peer set in this subsector is limited, comprising three comparables: Clippership, NetworkOcean, and Albacore. No public companies provide direct traded comparables for this frontier segment.

The stage-progression method is the applicable valuation approach given data constraints. Using total raised ($10M) as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $25M. This reflects the value creation expected between seed funding and Series A.

The triangulated valuation range is $21M to $29M, with a median of $25M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable methodology rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the limited private comp set available in the ocean-tech space, which constrains benchmarking reliability, and the absence of public trading comparables to anchor sector-level multiples. The estimate assumes Standard Subsea follows typical seed-stage progression; deviations in burn rate, market traction, or fundraising timeline could materially shift expectations. A more robust valuation would benefit from additional private peer data or clearer revenue/unit economics from the target.
